content:
I'm analyzing the architecture of the Awesome-Researcher-Agent project at /Users/wjx/code/Awesome-Researcher-Agent. I need to understand the testing strategy, code quality tooling, and CI/CD setup.

GOAL: Assess the project's quality infrastructure - test coverage, linting, CI/CD, documentation quality.

Find:
1. Test files and test patterns (pytest, unittest, test directory structure)
2. Test coverage configuration and current coverage levels
3. Linting/formatting configuration (ruff, black, mypy, flake8, etc.)
4. CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Makefile, tox, etc.)
5. Documentation (docstrings, README quality, API docs)
6. Pre-commit hooks if any
7. Type checking configuration (mypy, pyright)
8. Any integration tests or E2E tests

Return file paths with descriptions of what's found. Note gaps (missing tests, no CI, etc.).
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->
